architecture.summary: XGrammar is a constrained decoding engine for LLMs that enforces context-free grammar (CFG) compliance during token generation. It represents CFGs as byte-level pushdown automata (PDA) and accelerates mask generation by partitioning tokens into context-independent (prechecked during preprocessing) and context-dependent (resolved at runtime). The system maintains an adaptive token mask cache indexed by PDA stack state, executes context-dependent token validation via a persistent execution stack supporting fast branching and rollback, and co-schedules mask generation on CPU with LLM inference on GPU to overlap computation.

biblio.title: XGrammar: Flexible and Efficient Structured Generation Engine for Large Language Models

baseline.name: Outlines, llama.cpp, lm-format-enforcer (best baseline per task)

claims.dataset: JSON Schema and CFG (unconstrained JSON, XML, Python DSL)
claims.statement: XGrammar achieves up to 100x speedup over existing solutions in per-token latency for context-free grammar

baseline.name: vLLM with Outlines, llama.cpp with built-in grammar engine

claims.dataset: Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with JSON Schema and CFG tasks
claims.statement: XGrammar-integrated LLM serving engine achieves up to 80x speedup in end-to-end LLM serving with structured output on H100 GPU
Context-dependent tokens account for less than 1% (1134 out of 128k) for Llama-3.1 model using JSON grammar
Adaptive storage method reduces total memory usage to 0.2% (from 160 MB to 0.46 MB) for Llama-3.1 model and JSON grammar
Context expansion reduces context-dependent tokens by 90% (from 1134 to 120) for Llama-3.1 model and JSON grammar
Persistent execution stack with rollback reduces the number of characters checked across entire vocabulary to 30% for Llama-3.1 model and JSON grammar

Adaptive token mask cache optimization yields 248.6x speedup in per-token latency; cumulative with node merging (1.7x), rule inlining (4.4x), and context expansion (1.9x) achieves 65.776 ms to 0.018 ms reduction
XGrammar ensures 100% syntactic correctness on function calling (JSON schema) task vs. 62% without constraint, and 100% on XML code generation vs. 80% without constraint
